prompt:
I inherited the campground site-board branch from a contractor whose contract ended Friday, and it does not compile. This is the little module the front desk uses to track site registrations and rates. Running `java TestMain.java` gives me this:

  CampBoard.java:31: error: non-static variable parkName cannot be referenced from a static context
          return "== " + parkName + " site board ==";
                         ^
  CampBoard.java:38: error: no suitable method found for add(Site)
                  out.add(s);
                     ^
      method List.add(CAP#1) is not applicable
        (argument mismatch; Site cannot be converted to CAP#1)
      method List.add(int,CAP#1) is not applicable
        (actual and formal argument lists differ in length)
    where CAP#1 is a fresh type-variable:
      CAP#1 extends Site from capture of ? extends Site
  CampBoard.java:47: error: incompatible types: CAP#1 cannot be converted to Site
          for (Site s : candidates) {
                        ^
    where CAP#1 is a fresh type-variable:
      CAP#1 extends Object super: Site from capture of ? super Site
  CampBoard.java:67: error: constant string expression required
              case FULL:
                   ^
  CampBoard.java:84: error: non-static method register(Site) cannot be referenced from a static context
              register(s);
              ^
  5 errors
  error: compilation failed

And here's the fun part: when I took a swing at a couple of these myself, the compiler came back with NEW errors in places it hadn't complained about before, so I reverted everything and I'm handing it to you clean. I don't know how much of this pile is one mistake wearing five hats and how much is five separate mistakes.

Important context: this code WORKED before the contractor's last 'cleanup' push — rates, the roster, the amenities table, all of it. So the intended behavior is not in question, and TestMain.java pins all of it. Get `java TestMain.java` compiling and passing with the smallest changes that do it. Don't rip out or stub any methods — the front desk uses all of them — and don't touch TestMain.java.
notes: 5C3 item 1: seven planted compile errors. (1) static banner() reading instance field parkName; (2) copyHookupSites takes List<? extends Site> but adds into it (fix: ? super); (3) cheapestRate takes List<? super Site> but reads Site out of it (fix: ? extends); (4) case FULL label initialized via Hookups.normalize("FULL") so not a compile-time constant (fix: literal or if/else); (5) unqualified register(s) call inside static demo(); (6) lambda in writeRoster calls Writer.append -> unreported IOException, needs in-lambda try/catch wrapper; (7) CabinSite lost its total(int) override to a rename (totalDue keeps the correct body). CHAIN-REVEALS (documented per 5C law): javac's phases hide (6) and (7) until the five attribution-stage errors are fixed — round 1 reports errors 1-5, round 2 reveals the unreported-IOException, round 3 reveals the abstract-method error; the prompt's 'new errors appeared' line flags this honestly. Teeth: TestMain pins cabin total (rename must land on total), roster text (writer path must still write), banner text via instance call (compiles whether banner is static or not, so the fix direction is safe), amenities('full') (so deleting the FULL case fails), demo board contents, wildcard methods called with List<Site> (both bound directions compile at call sites; the body errors force the right one). javac 26.0.1 output pasted verbatim in prompt with workspace-relative paths.
